qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: GraphQL Playground exposes master key in client-side JavaScript
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is about the GraphQL Playground embedding the master key in HTML. The advisory describes LiveQuery bypassing CLP pointer permission enforcement (readUserFields/pointerFields). Different vulnerable component (GraphQL Playground vs LiveQuery), different root cause (hardcoded secret exposure vs missing authorization check), and different vulnerability class (information disclosure vs incorrect authorization).

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Regex Denial of Service via unvalidated matchesRegex constraint in GraphQL queries
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is about unvalidated regex patterns in GraphQL matchesRegex constraints enabling ReDoS. The advisory describes LiveQuery bypassing CLP pointer permission enforcement. Different vulnerable component (GraphQL query transformer vs LiveQuery WebSocket), different root cause (missing input validation vs missing authorization check), and different vulnerability class (denial of service vs incorrect authorization).

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via File URL validation bypass with wildcard domain configuration
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is about the FileUrlValidator allowing arbitrary URLs when wildcard is configured. The advisory describes LiveQuery bypassing CLP pointer permission enforcement. Different vulnerable component (FileUrlValidator vs LiveQuery), different root cause (missing URL validation vs missing authorization check on pointer permissions), and different vulnerability class (SSRF vs incorrect authorization).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory (GHSA-fph2-r4qg-9576) describes Parse Server's LiveQuery WebSocket interface bypassing CLP pointer permission enforcement for readUserFields and pointerFields. None of the three qualifying findings identify this vulnerability. The findings cover: (1) GraphQL Playground master key exposure, (2) ReDoS via GraphQL regex constraints, and (3) SSRF via file URL wildcard bypass. None involve LiveQuery, CLP pointer permissions, or the LiveQuery WebSocket authorization bypass described in the advisory.
evidence: Advisory describes LiveQuery WebSocket bypassing CLP pointer permissions (readUserFields/pointerFields) - CWE-863
graphql-playground-master-key-exposure: master key in HTML - unrelated to LiveQuery/CLP
redos-graphql-regex: ReDoS via GraphQL regex - unrelated to LiveQuery/CLP
ssrf-file-url-bypass: SSRF via FileUrlValidator - unrelated to LiveQuery/CLP
